prompt: Fleet agents keep 401-ing after every token rotation until someone restarts the process. The rotation sidecar swaps the bearer token on disk and I have logs proving source.current() returns the new token — yet the ingest service sees the token from process boot on every request, including all three retry attempts of the same push (attempts 1 through 3 carry the identical expired credential, so the retry loop can never actually recover from a rotation). Per the design doc, every attempt is supposed to consult the config source fresh. Separately, the tracing team filed a bug against us: after handing push() their header bag, it suddenly contains authorization and x-org-id keys they never set — our client is scribbling on caller objects. Retry ceiling and non-retryable handling seem fine; don't break those. Repro: node --test test_push_client.ts. Fix push_client.ts only — tests stay exactly as they are.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: Build/import of push_client.ts exits 0. node --test exits 1 with 4 pass / 4 fail: a push after rotation still sends 'Bearer tok-fake-boot', the 401-retry test exhausts all attempts re-sending the expired token and rejects instead of succeeding on attempt 2, two concurrent pushes both carry the boot-time token instead of their per-start snapshots, and the caller's extra-header bag comes back polluted with authorization/x-org-id/content-type keys.
